beats.activity: Wake up and morning routine at home in Eastlake.
beats.reasoning: Abigail can't start the day without a swim, so she wakes early. Energy is moderate (45/100 yesterday) but the ritual of morning swim will lift it.

beats.activity: Early morning swim at the pool before rain intensifies.
beats.reasoning: Non-negotiable morning ritual; the drizzle has just started and water will be quiet before the day picks up. Staying local in her neighborhood.

beats.activity: Quick breakfast and gear prep at home.
beats.reasoning: Post-swim routine: dry off, eat, pack fieldwork equipment before heading out in the rain.

beats.activity: Commute to Fremont fieldwork site on foot/bike.
beats.reasoning: Eastlake to Fremont is walkable/bikeable (~3 km); avoids SR-520 closure entirely. Rain is steady but manageable; marine biologist is used to wet conditions.

beats.activity: Fieldwork on Puget Sound: water sampling and sediment profiling.
beats.reasoning: Morning fieldwork window before SR-520 eastbound closure (9am–noon) would affect any cross-lake travel. Rain won't stop marine work; water temps and sediment are the focus.

beats.activity: Lunch at a nearby café, sheltering from the rain.
beats.reasoning: Short walk from Fremont; familiar spot from yesterday. Rain is still heavy (peaks at 2pm), so indoor lunch makes sense. Nostalgic comfort food fits her mood.
Walk/bike back from Wallingford to Fremont. Rain persisting; she's adapted to wet fieldwork.

beats.activity: Afternoon fieldwork: continued water profiling and data logging.
beats.reasoning: Rain tapers off by 2pm; afternoon is productive work time. SR-520 closure has cleared by now, but Abigail is happy at the waterfront.
Bike/walk back to Eastlake; weather clearing, moderate energy from solid fieldwork day.

beats.activity: Volunteer shift at the food bank.
beats.reasoning: Regular Tuesday volunteer slot; close to home, fits her community-oriented values. Energizes her despite moderate starting energy.

beats.activity: Dinner prep and meal at home.
beats.reasoning: Return home after volunteering; cook something simple and nourishing. Spontaneous evening ahead.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: vinyl spinning, notes on fieldwork, gentle reflection.
beats.reasoning: Nostalgic wind-down with records (Coltrane or similar), processing the day's observations. Spontaneous mood-following means staying home and introspecting.
7.75 hours of sleep; ends at 06:30 to allow for early morning swim tomorrow.

diary: Woke early and hit the pool before the rain got serious—those quiet laps always clear my head. The water was perfect, cold and still. Grabbed breakfast, then biked to Fremont despite the drizzle picking up. The Sound was gray and churning this morning, which actually made the sampling easier; fewer recreational boats in the way. Pulled some interesting sediment cores around 9:30, and the pH readings looked promising for the kelp restoration project. Ducked into Wallingford Deli for soup and a moment of warmth. Afternoon was solid work—logged data, took more profiles. Got home around two, still damp, and headed straight to Northwest Harvest. Sorting boxes of produce felt grounding after hours hunched over instruments. Made dinner, then spent the evening with some old records and my fieldwork notes. There's something about rain on the windows and Coltrane playing that makes you feel like you're exactly where you need to be.
thought_process: The rain actually worked in my favor today—calmer water meant cleaner samples, and the bridge closure didn't touch me since I stuck to Fremont and Eastlake. I prioritized the early swim because I knew the day would be intense; I needed that centering time. The fieldwork felt urgent, maybe because we're hitting critical months for the kelp baseline data. Volunteering at the food bank was partly genuine need—they're short-staffed—but also because I needed human contact that wasn't transactional. Work can isolate you. By evening, I was tired but settled, ready to sit with my thoughts and the data. The world feels precarious lately, and grounding myself in small rituals—the pool, the records, the food bank—feels like the only way I know how to push back.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
